sleep.title: Stick to a Sleep Schedule
sleep.tip: Go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on weekends. This helps regulate your body's internal clock.

sleep.title: Create a Restful Environment
sleep.tip: Keep your room dark, quiet, and cool. Consider using earplugs, an eye mask, or a white noise machine.

sleep.title: Limit Screen Time Before Bed
sleep.tip: Avoid phones, laptops, and tablets at least 30-60 minutes before bedtime. Blue light suppresses melatonin production.

sleep.title: Watch Your Caffeine Intake
sleep.tip: Avoid caffeine (coffee, tea, energy drinks) at least 6 hours before bedtime.

sleep.title: Establish a Wind-Down Routine
sleep.tip: Develop a relaxing pre-sleep routine: read a book, take a warm bath, or practice gentle stretching.

sleep.title: Avoid Heavy Meals Before Bed
sleep.tip: Don't eat large meals within 2-3 hours of bedtime. A light snack is fine if you're hungry.

sleep.title: Exercise Regularly
sleep.tip: Regular physical activity can help you fall asleep faster and enjoy deeper sleep, but avoid vigorous exercise close to bedtime.

sleep.title: Manage Worries
sleep.tip: Try writing down your concerns before bed. Journaling can help clear your mind and reduce bedtime anxiety.

sleep.title: Limit Naps
sleep.tip: If you nap, keep it to 20-30 minutes and avoid napping late in the afternoon.

sleep.title: Get Sunlight Exposure
sleep.tip: Spend time in natural sunlight during the day, especially in the morning. This helps maintain a healthy sleep-wake cycle.

stress.title: Practice Deep Breathing
stress.tip: Try the 4-7-8 technique: inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7 seconds, exhale for 8 seconds. Repeat 3-4 times.

stress.title: Break Tasks into Smaller Steps
stress.tip: Large assignments feel overwhelming. Break them into small, manageable tasks and focus on one at a time.

stress.title: Stay Physically Active
stress.tip: Even a 20-minute walk can significantly reduce stress hormones and boost your mood through endorphin release.

stress.title: Talk to Someone
stress.tip: Share your feelings with a trusted friend, family member, or counselor. You don't have to handle everything alone.

stress.title: Practice Mindfulness
stress.tip: Spend 5-10 minutes daily in mindfulness meditation. Focus on your breath and observe your thoughts without judgment.

stress.title: Limit Social Media
stress.tip: Constant comparison on social media increases stress. Set specific times for checking social media and stick to them.

stress.title: Get Enough Sleep
stress.tip: Sleep deprivation amplifies stress. Aim for 7-9 hours of quality sleep each night.

stress.title: Use the Pomodoro Technique
stress.tip: Study for 25 minutes, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 cycles, take a longer 15-30 minute break.

stress.title: Practice Gratitude
stress.tip: Write down 3 things you're grateful for each day. This simple practice can shift your mindset away from stress.

stress.title: Listen to Music
stress.tip: Calming music can lower cortisol levels and help you relax. Create a soothing playlist for stressful moments.

diet.title: Don't Skip Breakfast
diet.tip: Breakfast jumpstarts your metabolism and provides energy for morning classes. Even a banana and yogurt counts!

diet.title: Stay Hydrated
diet.tip: Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily. Keep a water bottle with you and sip throughout the day.

diet.title: Eat More Fruits and Vegetables
diet.tip: Aim for at least 5 servings of fruits and vegetables daily. They provide essential vitamins and keep you energized.

diet.title: Limit Processed and Junk Food
diet.tip: Chips, sugary snacks, and fast food provide empty calories. Replace with nuts, fruits, or whole grain options.

diet.title: Eat Regular Meals
diet.tip: Try to eat 3 balanced meals a day with healthy snacks in between. Skipping meals leads to energy crashes.

diet.title: Include Protein in Every Meal
diet.tip: Protein helps you stay full longer and supports brain function. Include eggs, lentils, chicken, or paneer.

diet.title: Reduce Sugar Intake
diet.tip: Excess sugar causes energy spikes and crashes. Swap sugary drinks for water, herbal tea, or fresh juice.

diet.title: Avoid Late-Night Eating
diet.tip: Eating heavy meals late at night disrupts sleep and digestion. If hungry, choose light options like warm milk or fruit.

diet.title: Plan Your Meals
diet.tip: Spending a few minutes planning meals for the week can help you eat healthier and save money.

diet.title: Eat Mindfully
diet.tip: Put away your phone during meals. Eating mindfully helps you enjoy your food and recognize when you're full.

screen_time.title: Follow the 20-20-20 Rule
screen_time.tip: Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This reduces eye strain significantly.

screen_time.title: Set Screen Time Limits
screen_time.tip: Use built-in screen time trackers to set daily limits, especially for social media and entertainment apps.

screen_time.title: No Screens Before Bed
screen_time.tip: Stop using screens at least 30-60 minutes before sleep. Blue light disrupts your melatonin production.

screen_time.title: Take Regular Breaks
screen_time.tip: Stand up, stretch, and walk around every 30-60 minutes during long study sessions.

screen_time.title: Use Night Mode / Blue Light Filters
screen_time.tip: Enable night mode or blue light filters on your devices, especially in the evening.

screen_time.title: Designate Screen-Free Zones
screen_time.tip: Keep your dining area and bedroom screen-free. This improves both eating habits and sleep quality.

screen_time.title: Be Intentional with Social Media
screen_time.tip: Before opening an app, ask yourself why. Set specific times for social media rather than mindless scrolling.

screen_time.title: Try Analog Alternatives
screen_time.tip: Replace some screen activities with offline alternatives: physical books, board games, outdoor activities.

screen_time.title: Adjust Display Settings
screen_time.tip: Reduce screen brightness, increase text size, and use dark mode to reduce eye strain during long sessions.

screen_time.title: Practice Digital Detox
screen_time.tip: Dedicate one day a week (or a few hours) to being completely screen-free. Notice how it affects your mood.
